question: Will Netflix (NFLX) close at $60-$70 on the final day of trading of the week of Feb 2 – Feb 6?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Netflix (NFLX) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Netflix (NFLX) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NFLX/history, published under "Historical Prices."

question: Transylvania Open, Qualification: Ekaterine Gorgodze vs Tamara Zidansek
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Ekaterine Gorgodze and Tamara Zidansek in the Transylvania Open, Qualification, scheduled for January 31 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Gorgodze' if Ekaterine Gorgodze advances against Tamara Zidansek.

This market will resolve to 'Zidansek' if Tamara Zidansek advances against Ekaterine Gorgodze.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Transylvania Open: Emma Raducanu vs Greet Minnen
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Emma Raducanu and Greet Minnen in the Transylvania Open, scheduled for February 1 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Raducanu' if Emma Raducanu advances against Greet Minnen.

This market will resolve to 'Minnen' if Greet Minnen advances against Emma Raducanu.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Transylvania Open: Miriam Bulgaru vs Olga Danilovic
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Miriam Bulgaru and Olga Danilovic in the Transylvania Open, scheduled for February 1 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Bulgaru' if Miriam Bulgaru advances against Olga Danilovic.

This market will resolve to 'Danilovic' if Olga Danilovic advances against Miriam Bulgaru.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NFLX/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) close at $8.00-$9.00 on the final day of trading of the week of Feb 2 – Feb 6?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Opendoor (OPEN) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Opendoor (OPEN) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OPEN/history, published under "Historical Prices."

question: Transylvania Open: Mayar Sherif vs Oleksandra Oliynykova
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Mayar Sherif and Oleksandra Oliynykova in the Transylvania Open, scheduled for February 1 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Sherif' if Mayar Sherif advances against Oleksandra Oliynykova.

This market will resolve to 'Oliynykova' if Oleksandra Oliynykova advances against Mayar Sherif.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to 'Potapova' if Anastasia Potapova advances against Lucia Bronzetti.

This market will resolve to 'Bronzetti' if Lucia Bronzetti advances against Anastasia Potapova.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will Palantir (PLTR) finish week of February 2 above $147?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PLTR/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OPEN/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 84.5 in Game 3?
description:
In the upcoming match between Power Rangers and BetBoom Team in the FISSURE Universe Episode 8 Playoffs, scheduled for January 30 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 3.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 3 is 85 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.
groupItemTitle: Total Kills Over/Under 84.5 in Game 3?

events.title: Dota 2: Power Rangers vs BetBoom Team (BO3) - FISSURE Universe Episode 8 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Power Rangers and BetBoom Team in the FISSURE Universe Episode 8 Playoffs, scheduled for January 30 at 2:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Power Rangers" if Power Rangers win the match against BetBoom Team.

This market will resolve to "BetBoom Team" if BetBoom Team win the match against Power Rangers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

question: Solana Up or Down - January 31, 6:00PM-6:15PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Solana price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the SOL/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/sol-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream SOL/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will the total number of TSA passengers for February 2 be less than 1,500,000?
description:
This market will resolve to the total number of TSA passengers reported on February 2, 2026. 

If the reported total number of TSA passengers falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve as soon as throughput data becomes available for the listed date. Any revisions published to data for dates December 1, 2025 and onward prior to the release of data for all dates within the listed range will be considered.

If data is not available for the listed date by March 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the daily checkpoint throughputs as measured by the US Transportation Security Administration (TSA), https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes. Should this URL change or move locations, a new URL on the tsa.gov domain will remain valid to resolve this market.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 86.5 in Game 3?
description:
In the upcoming match between Power Rangers and BetBoom Team in the FISSURE Universe Episode 8 Playoffs, scheduled for January 30 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 3.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 3 is 87 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.
groupItemTitle: Total Kills Over/Under 86.5 in Game 3?

events.title: Dota 2: Power Rangers vs BetBoom Team (BO3) - FISSURE Universe Episode 8 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Power Rangers and BetBoom Team in the FISSURE Universe Episode 8 Playoffs, scheduled for January 30 at 2:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Power Rangers" if Power Rangers win the match against BetBoom Team.

This market will resolve to "BetBoom Team" if BetBoom Team win the match against Power Rangers.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

This market will resolve to the total number of TSA passengers reported on February 7, 2026. 

If the reported total number of TSA passengers falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve as soon as throughput data becomes available for the listed date. Any revisions published to data for dates December 1, 2025 and onward prior to the release of data for all dates within the listed range will be considered.

If data is not available for the listed date by March 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the daily checkpoint throughputs as measured by the US Transportation Security Administration (TSA), https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes. Should this URL change or move locations, a new URL on the tsa.gov domain will remain valid to resolve this market.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 32.5 in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming match between LOS and paiN Gaming in the CBLOL Cup Regular Season, scheduled for January 31 at 1:00PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 33 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.
groupItemTitle: Total Kills Over/Under 32.5 in Game 1?

events.title: LoL: LOS vs paiN Gaming (BO1) - CBLOL Cup Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between LOS and paiN Gaming in the CBLOL Cup Regular Season, scheduled for January 31 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "LOS" if LOS win the match against paiN Gaming.

This market will resolve to "paiN Gaming" if paiN Gaming win the match against LOS.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

question: Solana Up or Down - January 31, 6:15PM-6:30PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Solana price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the SOL/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/sol-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream SOL/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Will Send Help or Iron Lung gross more on their opening weekend?
description:
Send Help and Iron Lung are scheduled for release on January 30, 2026. This market will resolve according to the film which grosses more domestically during their opening weekend in theaters. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on these movies' The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) pages will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (January 30 - February 1) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If both movies report the same gross for the 3-day opening weekend, this market will resolve in favor of the movie whose title comes first in alphabetical order. 

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

If there is no final data available by February 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

If the release date for one movie changes, this market will resolve to the movie which grosses more in its opening weekend once opening weekend figures for both movies are finalized. 

If the release of either movie is delayed beyond February 28, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Al Ittihad Saudi Club vs. Al Fayha Saudi Club: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming Saudi Pro League game between Al Ittihad Saudi Club and Al Fayha Saudi Club, scheduled for February 13 at 12:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Al Ittihad Saudi Club and Al Fayha Saudi Club combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market refers to the tennis match between Mayar Sherif and Oleksandra Oliynykova in the Transylvania Open, scheduled for February 1 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 24. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.

This market refers to the tennis match between Mara Gae and Maja Chwalinska in the Transylvania Open, Qualification, scheduled for January 31 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 23. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
All tiebreaks—including any Champions/Super tiebreak—count as one (1) game toward the total.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.
groupItemTitle: Transylvania Open, Qualification: Mara Gae vs Maja Chwalinska Match O/U 22.5

events.title: Transylvania Open, Qualification: Mara Gae vs Maja Chwalinska
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Mara Gae and Maja Chwalinska in the Transylvania Open, Qualification, scheduled for January 31 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Gae' if Mara Gae advances against Maja Chwalinska.

This market will resolve to 'Chwalinska' if Maja Chwalinska advances against Mara Gae.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
